name: Fairness
definition: To design AI systems that avoid biasness or discrimination against the users.
verbatim: AI systems must not discriminate based on age, gender, religion, or ethnicity. Training data must be diverse, representative, and free from biases. Developers must test AI models against diverse datasets and adopt bias-mitigation techniques. Continuous monitoring of algorithm performance is required to identify and address unintentional biases. This principle encompasses values of justice, respect for diversity, inclusion, equality, and avenues for redress. End users should adopt ethical checks to ensure AI output is fair and unbiased.
keyElements: Non-discrimination across protected characteristics (age, gender, religion, ethnicity)
Diverse and representative training data free from biases
Continuous monitoring of algorithm performance for unintentional biases
Ethical checks on AI output for fairness and impartiality
Avenues for redress when unfair outcomes occur

stakeholderObligations.developers: Ensure diverse training data, test models against diverse datasets, adopt bias-mitigation techniques
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Adopt ethical checks to ensure AI output is fair and unbiased
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Formulate policies that promote fairness and equal treatment in AI systems
